title: Changing Molecular Epidemiology of Hepatitis A Virus Infection, United States, 1996-2019.
author_string: Ramachandran, Sumathi, Xia, Guo-Liang, Dimitrova, Zoya, Lin, Yulin, Montgomery, Martha et al.
abstract: Hepatitis A virus (HAV) genotype IA was most common among strains tested in US outbreak investigations and surveillance during 1996-2015. However, HAV genotype IB gained prominence during 2016-2019 person-to-person multistate outbreaks. Detection of previously uncommon strains highlights the changing molecular epidemiology of HAV infection in the United States.
